title: French Invasion of Vietnam
summary: In 1858, the sounds of French colonizer gunfire echoed in Da Nang, beginning a difficult era as our nation faced a new enemy.

slides.text: In the mid-19th century, Western countries wanted to occupy other lands to seize resources. On September 1, 1858, French troops opened fire on the Son Tra Peninsula (Da Nang).
slides.imagePrompt: French warships with large chimneys smoke in Da Nang bay, firing cannons towards the shore, dramatic sky, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Our army and people in Da Nang, under the command of General Nguyen Tri Phuong, bravely fought back, preventing the French from advancing deep inland.
slides.imagePrompt: General Nguyen Tri Phuong commanding soldiers behind sandbags near the beach, cannons firing back, dust and energy, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: However, with modern weapons such as rifles and large ships, the French later redirected their attack towards Gia Dinh (modern-day Ho Chi Minh City).
slides.imagePrompt: French troops marching with rifles, tropical palm trees background, sense of challenge, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: From here, our people's resistance to protect the country entered a new stage, full of hardship but also extremely heroic.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese peasants sharpening bamboo stakes under the rain, faces full of determination, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Where did French colonialists first open fire to invade our country?
In what year did the French colonization of Vietnam begin?
